title: HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff CI hook (same-commit manual update enforcement)
goal: Build the `hbr-man-001-paired-diff.mjs` CI hook that enforces: any wired-surface change (Rust #[tauri::command], IPC channel, schema field, config key, CLI flag) MUST be accompanied by a corresponding ModelManual diff in the same commit, AND MANUAL_VERSION must bump. A commit that adds a Tauri command without updating the manual fails `just gov-check`.
expected_diff_shape: New script .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-man-001-paired-diff.mjs; justfile recipe + gov-check umbrella wiring; test fixture exercising pass/fail commits.
unblock_conditions: MT-010 ModelManual module with MANUAL_VERSION pub const merged so the diff check has something to grep.
implementation_notes: Per spec §10.15.7 (AC-MODEL-MANUAL-CI-HOOK). The script accepts CLI args `--base-ref <git ref>` (default HEAD~1) and `--target-ref <git ref>` (default HEAD). It runs `git diff --name-only <base>..<target>` to get the changed file list. It scans the changed Rust files for new/removed/renamed wired surfaces by regex-detecting: (a) `#[tauri::command]` attribute additions/removals; (b) IPC channel string-literal additions (pattern: `"kernel\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+"`); (c) struct field additions to types under `**/types.rs` matching IPC payload schemas (heuristic: structs that derive `Serialize, Deserialize`); (d) clap CLI flag additions (pattern: `#[arg(...)]` or `#[clap(...)]`); (e) config key additions to known config structs. For each detected wired-surface change, the script REQUIRES that the same diff also touches `src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/content.rs` AND that MANUAL_VERSION (in src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/mod.rs) bumps to a higher SemVer. If either condition fails, the script exits 2 with stderr JSONL `{file, surface_kind, name, reason}`. The bump rule: any wired-surface change forces at least a PATCH bump; ManualWorkflow or ManualSafetyConstraint changes force a MINOR bump; CommandReference removal forces a MAJOR bump. Justfile: add `hbr-man-001` recipe and wire into gov-check umbrella. Tests: fixture commit adds a #[tauri::command] without content.rs change -> exit 2; fixture commit adds the same command WITH content.rs change AND MANUAL_VERSION bump -> exit 0; fixture commit changes only renames a non-wired internal function -> exit 0 (no false positive). NOTE: This check runs in CI; locally operators run `just hbr-man-001` before commit.
risk_if_missed: Without paired-diff enforcement, manual drift creeps in commit-by-commit; HBR-MAN-001 becomes advisory and the in-product manual fossilizes.
validator_focus: False-positive rate: fixture commits that touch internal helpers (not wired surfaces) must NOT trigger; regex patterns are documented inline with their detection rationale.

phase2_remediation.lane: WP-KERNEL-004 Phase-2 A-cluster: HBR-MAN-001 config_key false-positive flood
remediated_findings: finding #1 (HIGH/CODE): detectConfigKeys in hbr-man-001-paired-diff.mjs over-fired on every changed `name:` line in any patch that merely contained a *Config/*Settings/*Options struct anywhere (455 false config_key surfaces on a single workflows.rs change). Rewritten to brace-track struct-definition bodies and emit config_key only for changed field lines INSIDE a config-relevant struct body. Struct literals, enum variants, and ordinary struct fields no longer false-fire.
phase2_remediation.deferred_or_residual: None. The MEDIUM/LOW findings folded into the same detector rewrite (the false-positive surface was the root concern); no spec-debt residual.
phase2_remediation.proof: node --test .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-man-001-paired-diff.test.mjs -> 10/10 pass, including two new MT-011 cases: a *Config-struct field IS flagged as config_key; ordinary struct fields and enum variant fields are NOT flagged even when the file contains a *Config struct (regression guard for the 455-flood).
phase2_remediation.validation_topology: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR_BATCH_MT_THEN_SPEC_V1 - no per-MT validator; awaiting Phase-2 IV re-judgment

minimum_controls: Test fixture includes a non-wired-surface change to assert zero false positives.
Regex patterns are documented with rationale next to each pattern so they can be tuned.
Bump rule is explicit (patch/minor/major) and the test enforces it.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: The paired-diff hook script exists, is wired into gov-check and the justfile, and its 8-test fixture suite passes cleanly (synthetic happy and failure paths both correct); however, the config_key detection regex over-fires on ordinary struct-literal expressions in any file/struct that mentions Config/Settings/Options, producing a large false-positive surface in real-history runs.
